## Potentially uninitialized local variable using the cleanup attribute

The variable [unescaped](1) may not be initialized here, but has a cleanup handler.

[Show more details](https://github.com/systemd/systemd/security/code-scanning/3035)
this function is so low-level. i think it's better to do error logging in caller, not in here
Please add more failure cases, passing an invalid value, `NULL`, and so on.

Where it is defined? Could you add a reference in the comment?

Also, are there no limit in the length?

i haven't found any limits in a standard, but per standard it's allowed to set limits and respond with http 400 in case of too long header names and values
standard allows US-ASCII characters for headers, can i use `is_ascii` there?
this list of symbols I've took from cloudflare website, thought they are relying on a RFC
Not sure the comments in https://stackoverflow.com/questions/686217/maximum-on-http-header-values is valid, but, then let's limit e.g. 8k. Our config parser allows 1M per line, that's too large.

> this list of symbols I've took from cloudflare website, thought they are relying on a RFC

Then, please mention that in the code.

https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7231#section-5.3.4
it's mentioned that Accept-Encoding header can be empty
An empty value string is allowed? If allowed, and `joined` is an empty string, then still `": "` is necessary?
How about the trailing space in that case?

https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2616#section-4.2
```
The field value MAY be preceded by any amount of LWS, though a single SP is preferred
```
Please also check the validity of the concatenated string with `header_value_is_valid()`.
concatenated header contains pair and header_value_is_valid checks validates value only
Please mention that no RFC seem to define that, and this is just for safety.
